source_docs.title: txci-ATAC-seq: a massive-scale single-cell technique to profile chromatin accessibility

oligos.name: Tn5ME-A
oligos.role: Tn5 transposon adapter A, transferred strand (Nextera Read 1 handle + mosaic end)
oligos.notes: Common (non-barcoded) adapter; annealed to Tn5MErev before loading Tn5. Table S4.

oligos.name: Tn5ME-B (barcoded)
oligos.role: Barcoded Tn5 transposon adapter B, transferred strand (partial i7 TruSeq/SBS handle + 8-bp Tn5 well barcode + mosaic end)

components.name: Partial TruSeq Read 2 / SBS handle
components.role: SBS / i7-primer binding site (also short-SBS binding site)

oligos.notes: Printed as CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNNAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (Table S4); the 8 N's are the Tn5 barcode. 96 barcode sequences in Additional file 5 Table S5. Annealed to Tn5MErev, one barcode per well of the iTSM plate.

oligos.name: Tn5MErev
oligos.role: Phosphorylated non-transferred mosaic-end strand; anneals to both Tn5ME-A and Tn5ME-B
oligos.notes: Table S4. 5' phosphate. Reverse complement of the 19-bp mosaic end.

oligos.name: Tn5 transposome A (Tn5ME-A / Tn5MErev)
oligos.role: Assembled Tn5 adapter-A duplex loaded into Tn5

oligos.name: Tn5 transposome B, barcoded (Tn5ME-B / Tn5MErev)
oligos.role: Assembled barcoded Tn5 adapter-B duplex loaded into Tn5 (one barcode per well)
oligos.notes: iTSM plate: 96-well plate pre-loaded with 5 µl of 500 nM pre-indexed Tn5 per well.

oligos.name: 10x Chromium gel-bead barcode primer (scATAC)
oligos.role: 10x GEM gel-bead primer carrying P5 + 16-bp cell barcode + Nextera Read 1; anneals to the A-end mosaic end during in-droplet amplification

components.name: Nextera Read 1 (Read1N)
components.role: Read 1 primer; anneals to A-end mosaic end

oligos.notes: Provided on the 10x gel bead (Chromium Next GEM Single Cell ATAC v1.1, CG000209 Rev D). Exact bead barcode not printed in the txci documents; the 16-bp barcode is sequenced as the i5 index read (16 cycles).

oligos.name: Customized i7 TruSeq index primer
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR primer: adds P7 + 8-bp i7 sample/lane barcode via the B-end partial SBS handle

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 primer
components.role: anneals to B-end partial SBS handle (CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT)

oligos.notes: 25 µM, 2.5 µl per 10x library; replaces the 10x Single Index N Set A. Only the partial handle CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT is printed verbatim in the txci Tn5ME-B; the flanking P7 and Read 2 constants follow the standard Illumina TruSeq i7 primer. 8-bp barcode sequences in Additional file 5 Table S7.

oligos.name: Short SBS oligo
oligos.role: Reverse primer spiked into the GEM/loading buffer (5 µM) to drive exponential in-droplet amplification and out-compete Tn5 barcode swapping
oligos.notes: Final txci-ATAC-seq protocol reagent (protocols.io LBS buffer; note 'SBS oligo sequence (5'-3') is CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT'). Same sequence as the partial SBS handle at the 5' end of Tn5ME-B.

oligos.name: Full SBS primer (barcoded)
oligos.role: Barcoded full-length SBS/Read 2 primer tested for in-droplet exponential amplification (SBS-primer blocking strategy)
oligos.notes: 25 µM. Sequence provided only in Additional file 5 Table S8 (not reproduced in the supplied text); lowercase letters denote the barcode. In the final protocol this was replaced by the un-barcoded short SBS oligo.

oligos.name: Blocking oligo
oligos.role: Barcode-swap blocking strategy: reverse complement of the Tn5 adapter bearing a 3' inverted dideoxy-dT, to prevent free Tn5 adapters priming amplification
oligos.notes: 100 µM DNA oligo with a 3' inverted dT, spiked into the GEM master mix. Exact sequence in Additional file 5 Table S8 (not printed). Tested strategy, not used in the final protocol.

oligos.name: Decoy DNA (duplex)
oligos.role: Barcode-swap blocking strategy: unamplifiable duplex DNA added post-tagmentation to exhaust excess free Tn5
oligos.notes: 50 µM; strands A and B annealed to form a duplex. Sequences in Additional file 5 Table S8 (not printed). Tested strategy, not used in the final protocol.

oligos.name: Nextera Read 1 sequencing primer (Read1N)
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer (also used for the i5 index read of the 10x cell barcode)
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera Read 1 primer; identical to the Tn5ME-A transferred strand.

oligos.name: TruSeq Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer (reads the 8-bp Tn5 barcode, mosaic end, then genomic insert)

oligos.name: i7 index sequencing primer
oligos.role: i7 index read primer (reads the 8-bp sample index)

final_library.source_label: txci-ATAC-seq (Zhang et al., Genome Biology 2024; 10.1186/s13059-023-03150-1) — 10x Chromium Next GEM scATAC v1.1 + 96-plex barcoded Tn5
annotation_lines: [CELL_BARCODE:16] = 10x cell barcode (16 bp, read as i5 index)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT = Mosaic End (reverse complement, B end)
[TN5_BARCODE:8] = Tn5 well barcode (8 bp, read as first 8 bp of Read 2)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = Partial TruSeq Read 2 / SBS handle (reverse complement)

evidence.locator: txci-ATAC-seq (Zhang et al., Genome Biology 2024; 10.1186/s13059-023-03150-1) — 10x Chromium Next GEM scATAC v1.1 + 96-plex barcoded Tn5

library_generation.title: Load Tn5 with barcoded adapters
library_generation.summary: Anneal Tn5MErev to Tn5ME-A and to each barcoded Tn5ME-B, then load into Tn5 to build common (A) and well-barcoded (B) transposomes.
library_generation.note: Tn5ME-A and each 8-bp-barcoded Tn5ME-B are separately annealed to the phosphorylated Tn5MErev, then loaded onto Tn5 (iTSM plate: 500 nM pre-indexed Tn5 per well). Each of 96 wells carries a unique Tn5 barcode.
library_generation.product:
Transposome A (common):
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
              3'- TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5' (5'Phos)

Transposome B (barcoded, 1 of 96):
5'- CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT[TN5_BARCODE:8]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
                            3'- TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5' (5'Phos)

library_generation.title: 96-plex barcoded tagmentation of nuclei
library_generation.summary: Barcoded Tn5 tagments accessible chromatin in each well (37 °C, 60 min), inserting adapter A on one side and the well-barcoded adapter B on the other.
library_generation.note: Each well's Tn5 inserts its unique 8-bp barcode into open chromatin. A productive fragment carries a common A end and a barcoded B end; the 9-bp gaps at the mosaic-end junctions are filled during the first cycle of in-droplet amplification. Nuclei from all 96 wells are then pooled.
library_generation.product:
Productive tagmented fragment (transferred-strand / top strand after gap fill):
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[TN5_BARCODE:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACG -3'
    |____ Nextera R1 + ME ____|        |__ ME(rc) __|          |__ SBS handle(rc) __|

library_generation.title: 10x GEM encapsulation & in-droplet barcoding
library_generation.summary: Pooled nuclei are overloaded onto the 10x Chromium; inside each droplet the gel-bead primer (P5 + 16-bp cell barcode + Read1N) anneals to the A-end mosaic end and extends, and the short SBS oligo primes the B end for exponential amplification.
library_generation.note: The gel-bead primer adds P5 and the 16-bp 10x cell barcode to the A end. Spiking in the short SBS oligo converts the normally linear in-droplet reaction into exponential amplification, out-competing Tn5 barcode swapping. 8 GEM PCR cycles (lung/liver).
library_generation.product:
gel-bead primer annealing:
|--5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[CELL_BARCODE:16]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------->
                                                   3'- AGCAGCCGTCGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[CDNA]... -5'

product (top strand):
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[CELL_BARCODE:16]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[TN5_BARCODE:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACG -3'

library_generation.title: Sample Index PCR → final library
library_generation.summary: After the GEMs are broken and cleaned up, the customized i7 TruSeq primer anneals to the B-end SBS handle and adds an 8-bp i7 sample index + P7 (5 cycles), completing the dual-P5/P7 library.
library_generation.note: A P5 primer and the barcoded i7 TruSeq primer amplify the fragment, appending the i7 sample index and P7. Double-sided size selection follows. The resulting library is dual-indexed (i7 sample + i5 10x barcode) and additionally carries the Tn5 barcode within Read 2.
library_generation.product:
i7 TruSeq primer annealing at the B end:
5'- ...[TN5_BARCODE:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
                       <----- GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT ... (i7 TruSeq primer) ... -5'

Final library (top strand 5'->3'):
AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[CELL_BARCODE:16]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[TN5_BARCODE:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG

library_sequencing.note: Sequences the accessible-chromatin genomic insert from the A end. Primer anneals to the A-end Read1N/mosaic-end region and extends into the insert (50 cycles in the earliest cell-line runs; 51 in lung/liver runs).

Reads the 8-bp i7 sample/lane index that distinguishes 10x lanes. Primer anneals at the B-end TruSeq/SBS handle and extends into the sample index. 10 cycles were sometimes run to co-sequence bulk libraries, but the txci index is only 8 bp.
Reads the 16-bp 10x GEM/cell barcode as the i5 index. The Read1N primer anneals to the A-end Read1N region and the barcode is read back toward P5.

Reads the 8-bp Tn5 well barcode, then the 19-bp mosaic end (CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT), then the genomic insert from the B end. Preprocessing moves the first 8 bp to the header and trims the first 27 bp; cycles 9-27 may be run as dark cycles (or balanced with PhiX/bulk ATAC) to offset the fixed Tn5MErev bases (77 cycles in early runs; 78 in lung/liver).
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...[INSERT]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[TN5_BC:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]... -3'
3'- ...[INSERT]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA[TN5_BC:8]TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]... -5'
  reads INSERT|ME|Tn5_BC <-------3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5' (Read 2 primer, binds B-end handle)

title: txci-ATAC-seq
description: txci-ATAC-seq (Ten-X-compatible Combinatorial Indexing ATAC-seq) is a massive-scale single-cell chromatin-accessibility assay that couples 96-well plate-based barcoded Tn5 tagmentation ("molecular pre-indexing") with 10x Chromium droplet (GEM) barcoding. Accessible chromatin is tagmented in each well by a Tn5 loaded with a common Tn5ME-A adapter and a well-specific 8-bp-barcoded Tn5ME-B adapter; nuclei are pooled, overloaded onto the 10x scATAC platform, and each fragment additionally receives a 16-bp GEM/cell barcode and, in a final PCR, an 8-bp i7 sample index. The combination of i7 sample barcode + 16-bp 10x barcode + 8-bp Tn5 barcode deconvolutes up to ~200,000 nuclei per lane; Read 1 and Read 2 capture the genomic insert while the barcodes are read as the i5 index, i7 index, and the first 8 bp of Read 2.

reference.label: txci-ATAC-seq: a massive-scale single-cell technique to profile chromatin accessibility

original_publication.title: txci-ATAC-seq: a massive-scale single-cell technique to profile chromatin accessibility

throughput.summary: Up to ~200,000 nuclei indexed per single 10x lane across up to 96 samples (~22-fold over standard 10x Chromium scATAC at matched collision rate); 449,953 nuclei profiled across human/mouse brain, human/mouse lung, and mouse liver. Simulation: max ~194,970 usable cells at 470,680-nuclei input with 96 Tn5 barcodes.
throughput.cells: up to ~200,000 nuclei/lane (theoretical max ~194,970 usable at 470,680 input)
throughput.dna: chromatin-accessibility (ATAC) fragments; median estimated library complexity ~25,000 unique fragments/cell

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (nuclei-per-droplet modeled as Poisson; in-droplet collision rate computed as the probability of a duplicate Tn5 barcode within a droplet; observed collision rates corrected via Bloom 2018)

other.label: Collision rate (SBS primer, true barnyard cell lines)
Barcode-swap fix
other.value: Short SBS oligo enabling exponential in-droplet amplification (vs Decoy DNA / Blocking oligo strategies)

other.label: Data
other.value: GEO GSE245957 (brain), GSE231708 (cell line/lung/liver); dbGaP phs003497.v1 (human cortex)

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed on the combination of the i7 sample index, the 16-bp 10x GEM barcode, and the 8-bp Tn5 well barcode; the Tn5 barcode (first 8 bp of Read 2) is moved to the read header and the first 27 bp (8-bp barcode + 19-bp mosaic end) are trimmed, reads are adapter/quality-trimmed and aligned to a hybrid human/mouse genome, deduplicated per combined barcode, then peaks are called and cells are clustered and annotated by label transfer, with barnyard collision-rate estimation and Poisson-based cell-recovery simulation used to deconvolute over-loaded droplets.

nodes.label: Annotate cell types by label transfer

data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson model for nuclei-per-droplet occupancy and cell-recovery simulation; negative-binomial (edgeR quasi-likelihood F-test) for differential accessibility.
